title: papaver alpinum subsp. suaveolens
intro: Papaver alpinum subsp. suaveolens is a subspecies of the Alpine Poppy, a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as skin irritations, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Papaver alpinum subsp. suaveolens include reducing pain and inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties. The herbal preparations of this plant include making teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as using it in salves and creams for topical application.
